_comment: Autonomous original-post decision-maker (src/triggers/ai_manager.py) -- no reposts, those are manual-only now. Deliberately a lighter, quality-over-volume profile, now split into a day/night posting window (Europe/Brussels time) instead of one flat daily cap: 06:00-23:00 is the day window (day_max_posts hard cap, with a small day_tag_exception_reserve on top reserved for genuinely urgent JUST IN/BREAKING posts only), 23:00-06:00 is the night window (night_max_posts -- a couple of high-value 'magnet' posts to keep the feed alive overnight, not a real posting cadence, plus its own small night_tag_exception_reserve so genuinely urgent JUST IN/BREAKING news still posts overnight instead of waiting for the day window). Within the day window, posts are paced to spread across the whole window rather than front-loading the cap by mid-morning (see ai_manager.py's _paced_cap_for) -- confirmed live that without pacing, a burst of Claude calls early in the day could exhaust the day cap by ~10am and leave the rest of the day silent. Each Claude call still produces a BATCH of up to posts_per_batch posts -- the first fires immediately, the rest queue and drain one per subsequent hourly run, and the pacing gate applies at drain time too so an over-queued batch still spreads out naturally rather than draining back-to-back. CONTEXT-tagged posts (routine market-recap/evergreen filler) are explicitly deprioritized in the prompt in favor of genuinely newsy JUST IN/BREAKING/NEWS/CRYPTO/AI content. No images and no links, by deliberate choice -- second_part_every_n_posts nudges Claude's own per-post second_part decision instead.
